text: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) has emerged as a cornerstone technology for 5G-Advanced (5G-A) and 6G networks due to its ability to improve spectral efficiency and reduce hardware overhead. By unifying high-accuracy sensing with robust data transmission, ISAC is poised to enable a wide range of intelligent applications. Since the underlying waveform dictates the upper bounds of both sensing and communication performance, this article provides a comprehensive review of ISAC signals through the lens of modern mobile communication systems. We first examine signal design strategies tailored for the evolving architectures of 5G, 5G-A, and 6G. We then explore essential radar signal processing techniques applicable to ISAC, focusing on channel information matrix estimation, spectral line estimation, and super-resolution algorithms. Furthermore, we address critical signal optimization challenges, including peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) reduction, interference management, and adaptive waveform design. By synthesizing current advancements and methodologies, this review serves as a roadmap for future research and development of ISAC signals in next-generation mobile networks.
